market_context: US stock futures are declining sharply due to escalating Iran conflict tensions and rising Treasury yields near 4.04%, with E-mini S&P 500 down 1.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures off nearly 2%, and Russell 2000 futures lower by 2.6%[1][2]. Pre-market movers include gains in Venture Global (VG) up 17.44% on strong earnings and LNG deal, Coherent (COHR) up 15.44% on analyst upgrades, while losers like Estée Lauder (EL) down 8.48% and Elevance Health (ELV) down 8.10% on leadership changes weigh on sentiment[2]. Key earnings today from AutoZone (AZO), Sea (SE), Ross Stores (ROST), and CrowdStrike (CRWD) after close could influence sector rotations amid higher-for-longer rate pressures[2].

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures are down 1.5% amid Iran conflict escalation and 10-year Treasury yield at 4.04%, signaling broad market pressure on growth stocks[1][2]. Elevated yields from Middle East tensions and inflation worries are likely to persist, driving end-of-day declines in SPY[2]. Upcoming earnings may not offset macro headwinds today.

Nasdaq 100 futures are off nearly 2%, more than S&P, as higher yields hit growth-focused tech harder amid renewed inflation fears tied to energy costs[2]. Geopolitical risks from Iran are amplifying risk-off sentiment in tech-heavy QQQ[1]. CrowdStrike and Broadcom earnings loom but pre-market tone suggests downside continuation.

Venture Global jumped 17.44% pre-market on strong Q4/full-year earnings and a new long-term LNG deal, providing momentum amid energy cost worries from Middle East tensions[2]. LNG export focus positions it resilient to broader market slide[2]. Gains likely hold through close absent negative catalysts.

Elevance Health slipped 8.10% pre-market following an executive leadership transition and advisor role update, eroding investor confidence in healthcare sector[2]. Broader futures weakness adds downward pressure without countering news[1][2]. Decline probable at end-of-day.
